channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ancient Star Cluster Reveals Early Universe Secrets via Hubble Telescope
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured an image of NGC 6426, a globular cluster in the Milky Way's outer halo, celebrating the U.S. 250th anniversary. This 13-billion-year-old cluster offers insig...

item.title: Canada's Football Journey Marks a New Era After World Cup Exit
item.description: Canada's national football team expressed gratitude to fans after their World Cup elimination, marking a transformative moment for the sport in the country. Despite a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in the roun...

item.title: Philippine personalities congratulate Alex Eala for her historic Wimbledon achievement
item.description: Several well-known personalities in the Philippines expressed their congratulations and admiration for Filipino tennis star Alex Eala following her historic achievement at Wimbledon. Her performance i...

item.title: Federal Government Expands Health Workforce with 37,000 New Recruits
item.description: The Nigerian federal government has announced the recruitment of 37,000 health workers since 2023 as part of its efforts to strengthen the healthcare system. This initiative, detailed in a statement b...

item.title: BC's Left Lane Rules: Passing vs. Driving Lanes Explained
item.description: The article discusses British Columbia's traffic laws regarding the use of the left lane on highways. While there is no explicit law reserving the left lane solely for passing, drivers are required to...

item.title: Sports Minister Defends Player After Criticism Over Political Remarks
item.description: Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture Gayton McKenzie has publicly defended Bafana Bafana midfielder Teboho Mokoena following backlash from ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula. Mokoena, who scored cruc...

item.title: Canada reflects on World Cup journey after Morocco defeat
item.description: Canada's football team concluded their historic World Cup campaign with an emotional message to supporters, acknowledging the transformative impact of their tournament run despite losing to Morocco in...

item.title: Canada exits 2026 World Cup after 3-0 defeat to Morocco in Round of 16
item.description: Canada’s historic World Cup campaign came to an end in the Round of 16 after a 3-0 loss to Morocco on July 4, 2026. The match, played in the first-ever World Cup appearance for Canada, saw the team fa...

item.title: Supreme Court Ruling on Trump's Birthright Citizenship Ban Sparks MAGA Outrage
item.description: The article discusses the reaction of MAGA supporters to the Supreme Court's decision to strike down Donald Trump's birthright citizenship ban, which was deemed racist and xenophobic. It highlights th...

item.title: Quadcopter Attack in Lower South Waziristan Leaves 3 Girls and 4 Family Members Injured
item.description: A suspected quadcopter drone attack in Lower South Waziristan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, resulted in the deaths of three girls and injuries to four family members, including one woman and three boys. The in...

item.title: Young Players' Growth Highlighted in Gilas Pilipinas' FIBA World Cup Journey
item.description: Despite a heartbreaking 106-102 double overtime loss to New Zealand in the FIBA World Cup Asian Qualifiers, Gilas Pilipinas’ young players showcased significant growth, carrying the team's offensive l...

item.title: NSW's strategy for the NRL Origin decider: Focus on weather advantage and limiting key players
item.description: The article discusses Joey and Danika's analysis of the upcoming NRL Origin decider, focusing on NSW's strategic approach. With expected wet weather, the Blues' plan leverages the conditions to their ...

item.title: Kenya Invests Sh1 Trillion in Digital Infrastructure Expansion
item.description: The Kenyan government has announced a major investment of Sh1 trillion aimed at expanding telecommunications infrastructure and strengthening the digital economy. This initiative focuses on improving ...

item.title: Africa's Climate Resilience Lessons for a Super El Niño
item.description: A super El Niño, characterized by unusually warm Pacific Ocean temperatures, is expected to cause severe weather impacts globally. The article highlights Africa's experience managing climate volatilit...

item.title: Nigel Farage Faces Leadership Challenges and Financial Scrutiny in Reform UK
item.description: The article details the growing internal turmoil within Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, as he faces mounting pressure from financial scandals, factional disputes, and strategic missteps. Key issues in...

item.title: Mariners dominate Blue Jays in 11-0 shutout as Gilbert and Arozarena shine
item.description: The Seattle Mariners delivered a commanding 11-0 victory over the Toronto Blue Jays on Saturday, showcasing their offensive prowess and pitching depth. Logan Gilbert (7-5) dominated the game, surrende...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Sara Duterte May Be Shortened if Evidence Sufficient
item.description: House prosecution panel adviser Robert Ace Barbers stated that the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte could be shortened if the evidence presented is deemed sufficient. He emphasized tha...

item.title: Mumbai Family Still Haunted by Tree Branch Accident After 8 Years
item.description: Prakash Nayak, 43, and his family in Mumbai still live in fear of falling tree branches eight years after a fatal incident that claimed his 91-year-old aunt Leela Sukhi's life. The accident occurred o...

item.title: Corporate Security and National Security Intersections in Tech Regulation
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between corporate security, national security, and government regulation of technology companies. It highlights debates over whether private entities shou...

item.title: Arusha Hosts International Art and Tourism Festival to Boost Creative Industry
item.description: Arusha is set to host a major international art and tourism festival in October 2026, bringing together over 1,500 artists and traders from around the world. The event, organized under the Tanzania In...
